title: Prime Check

content.problemStatement: Given an integer **N** less than 101010, determine whether the number **N** is prime. If it is prime, print **prime number**; otherwise, print **non-prime number**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N on its own line.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "prime number" if N is prime, otherwise print "non-prime number" on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 5 has no divisors other than 1 and itself, so it is prime.
4 is divisible by 2, therefore it is not prime.

hidden.label: uniform case
hidden.expectedOutput:
non-prime number
non-prime number
non-prime number
non-prime number
non-prime number

hidden.label: uniform case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
prime number
non-prime number
prime number
non-prime number

hidden.expectedOutput:
prime number
prime number
prime number

hidden.label: structured case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
non-prime number
non-prime number
prime number

solution.approach: For each number we need to decide whether it has any divisor other than 1 and itself. A number N < 2 is not prime. For N ≥ 2 we test divisibility by every integer i from 2 up to √N. If any i divides N, the number is composite; otherwise it is prime. This trial‑division works in O(√N) time per test case, which is easily fast enough because N ≤ 101009 (√N ≤ 318).
solution.timeComplexity: O(√N) per test case, O(T·√N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input and output buffers.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- > 0){long n=Long.parseLong(br.readLine().trim());if(n<2){sb.append("non-prime number\n");continue;}boolean prime=true;for(long i=2;i*i<=n;i++){if(n%i==0){prime=false;break;}}sb.append(prime?"prime number":"non-prime number").append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
